name: GraphQl

unions.name: Request
unions.comment: Requests contains the query and the variables of each GraphQl requests.

values.name: addVariables
values.comment:
Add variables to a requests. Useful when defining variables in your GraphQL request.

    object []
      |> flip (query "https://example.com") decoder
      |> addVariables []
      |> send msg
values.type: List ( String, Value ) -> Request a -> Request a

values.name: int
values.comment:
Generate an argument, to use with `withArgument`.

    fied "user"
      |> withArgument "id" (GraphQl.int 12)

Turns into:

    user(id: 12)

values.name: named
values.comment:
Generate a Value with a name.

    named "MySuperRequest"
      [ field "user" ]

Turns into:

    query MySuperRequest {
      user
    }

values.name: object
values.comment:
Generate a Value, from a list of fields.

    object
      [ field "user" ]

Turns into:

    query {
      user
    }

values.name: query
values.comment:
Entry of every GraphQL values to turn them into requests, which can be launched!

    object []
      |> flip (query "https://example.com") decoder
      |> send msg
values.type: String -> Value -> Decoder a -> Request a

values.name: send
values.comment: Actually send the GraphQL request! Generates a Cmd, to give to Elm in your update.
values.type: (Result Error a -> msg) -> Request a -> Cmd msg

values.name: string
values.comment:
Generate an argument, to use with `withArgument`.

    fied "user"
      |> withArgument "id" (GraphQl.string "12")

Turns into:

    user(id: "12")

values.name: type_
values.comment:
Generate an argument, to use with `withArgument`.
Generate a type in GraphQL.

    fied "user"
      |> withArgument "id" (GraphQl.type_ "INT")

Turns into:

    user(id: INT)

values.name: variable
values.comment:
Generate an argument, to use with `withArgument`.
You don't have to handle the $ sign.

    fied "user"
      |> withArgument "id" (GraphQl.variable "id")

Turns into:

    user(id: $id)

values.name: withAlias
values.comment:
Add an alias to a Field.

    field "user"
      |> withAlias "currentUser"
      |> withSelectors
        [ field "id"
        , field "first_name"
        , field "last_name"
        ]

Turns into:

    currentUser: user {
      id
      first_name
      last_name
    }

values.name: withArgument
values.comment:
Add an argument to a Field.

    field "user"
      |> withArgument "id" (GraphQl.string "12")
      |> withSelectors
        [ field "id"
        , field "first_name"
        , field "last_name"
        ]

Turns into:

    user(id: "12") {
      id
      first_name
      last_name
    }

values.name: withSelectors
values.comment:
Add selectors to a Field.

    field "user"
      |> withSelectors
        [ field "id"
        , field "first_name"
        , field "last_name"
        ]

Turns into:

    user {
      id
      first_name
      last_name
    }

values.name: withVariable
values.comment:
Add a variable to a Field.

    field "user"
      |> withVariable "id" "id"

Turns into:

    user(id: $id)
